title: Iran ceasefire narrative vs Hormuz shipping data -- May 10 2026
narrative: President Trump has repeatedly declared the Iran ceasefire intact as of May 2026, including in a May 8 letter to Congress stating no further exchange of fire occurred. The administration simultaneously claims credit for protecting ships in the Gulf while announcing pauses in operations to pursue diplomatic deals. Officials insist the Strait of Hormuz is secure and commercial shipping can resume.
telemetry: AIS vessel tracking data (Strait of Hormuz Live Tracker, hormuztracking.com) shows near-zero commercial traffic through the strait for weeks since the February 28 2026 closure
NBC News graphic (May 2026) confirms ship traffic at near-standstill
Reuters (May 4) reports Trump moves backfired — no surge in merchant traffic despite announcements
US strikes on Iranian tankers confirmed May 6-8 (Times of Israel, NY Times Live) — directly contradicts ceasefire intact narrative
Iran Joint Command accuses US of violating truce by targeting tankers near Hormuz
Only 1-2 vessels have completed the crossing since closures began (Guardian, March 2026)
War risk insurance costs 20x pre-war levels (Al Jazeera, April 2026)
Chevron CEO publicly warns of jet fuel crisis — physical evidence of ongoing disruption
analysis:
Official sources say one thing. The evidence says another. Headline: Ceasefire Intact, Shipping Dead. Contrast Trumps public statements claiming the strait is open and ceasefire holding against live AIS vessel tracking data showing zero commercial traffic. Use the Chevron jet fuel warning as economic corroboration. Open with the vessel tracker as primary evidence, layer in contradictory official statements chronologically. Tone: adversarial OSINT, let the data speak, cite sources precisely.

• AIS vessel tracking data (Strait of Hormuz Live Tracker, hormuztracking.com) shows near-zero commercial traffic through the strait for weeks since the...
• NBC News graphic (May 2026) confirms ship traffic at near-standstill
• Reuters (May 4) reports Trump moves backfired — no surge in merchant traffic despite announcements
• US strikes on Iranian tankers confirmed May 6-8 (Times of Israel, NY Times Live) — directly contradicts ceasefire intact narrative

The pattern in the telemetry — 8 independent data points — points in a consistent direction that the official narrative does not acknowledge. When the official framing and the physical evidence are in contradiction, the evidence is the more reliable signal. The gap between what officials claim and what the data shows is the story.
verdict: [SIPHONED VERDICT]: Headline: Ceasefire Intact, Shipping Dead. Contrast Trumps public statements claiming the strait is open and ceasefire holding against live AIS vessel tracking data showing zero commercial traffic. Us
